title: ferray-ufunc: every operation allocates a new Vec — no out= parameter
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy ufuncs accept out= to write results into a pre-allocated array, avoiding allocation on hot paths: np.add(a, b, out=c). ferray-ufunc always allocates a new Vec and wraps it in a new Array. The helpers (unary_float_op, binary_float_op, etc.) all do Vec::with_capacity + collect/loop. For repeated operations on same-shaped arrays (e.g. in a loop, or chained operations), this means O(n) allocation per call. Add out= variants (or _into suffix) for at least the core arithmetic and transcendental functions. The out array could be passed as &mut Array<T,D> and the kernel writes directly into its buffer.
